text.title: django-serializers Failure Modes
text.summary: Five concrete antipatterns mapped to rules in `01-core-rules.xml`. Each has a symptom that can be detected in production logs or static analysis.

antipattern.description: Single `ModelSerializer` with `fields = "__all__"` reused for both create and detail endpoints.
antipattern.symptom: Pen test report flags password hash, `is_staff`, `last_login` in JSON responses; new model fields appear in responses without code changes.
antipattern.root-cause: Convenience: one serializer to write. `__all__` includes every field forever, including those added later.
antipattern.fix: Split into `EntityCreateRequest` / `EntityResponse`. Both inherit from `serializers.Serializer`. List exposed fields explicitly.

antipattern.description: `validate_email`, `validate()`, or `to_internal_value()` querying the database or calling a service.
antipattern.symptom: Inconsistent state when the wrapping service transaction rolls back; uniqueness violation arriving as 500 instead of 400; duplicate side-effects on retry.
antipattern.root-cause: "Validation feels like the right place" — but business invariants belong inside the service transaction so rollback cleans up.
antipattern.fix: Move the uniqueness check into the service. Validation stays shape-only. Catch `IntegrityError` in the service and re-raise as `ValidationError`.

antipattern.description: `exclude = ["password", "secret"]` used as the security boundary on an output serializer.
antipattern.symptom: A new sensitive field (`reset_token`, `mfa_secret`) is added to the model; nobody updates exclude; field leaks on next deploy.
antipattern.root-cause: Excludelist is fail-open. Authors must remember to update it; review usually misses.
antipattern.fix: Replace with an explicit allowlist on a `Serializer` (not ModelSerializer). Add a unit test asserting the field set equals the expected names.

antipattern.symptom: Empty strings silently accepted as valid names; clients sending `null` produce inconsistent behaviour; DRF major-version upgrade changes API behaviour.
antipattern.root-cause: Trusting DRF defaults that are not memorised and change across versions.
antipattern.fix: Declare every option explicitly. Run `ruff` with a custom rule (or grep) to flag fields lacking `required=`.

antipattern.description: Nested serializer uses `PrimaryKeyRelatedField(queryset=Model.objects.all())` or `RelatedSerializer(many=True)` over an unprefetched manager.
antipattern.symptom: N+1 reappears during render; selector-level N+1 test passes but endpoint test (with full serialisation) does not.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treats DRF render layer as free. Each related access on an unprefetched manager re-queries.
antipattern.fix: Ensure the parent queryset comes from a selector that includes the necessary `select_related` / `prefetch_related`. Use `serializers.PrimaryKeyRelatedField(read_only=True)` for ids; for nested objects iterate already-prefetched data via the explicit Output serializer.
